patch_instructions: In WIN id="067" detail_evidence, find the EXACT old substring (verified unique — occurs exactly once, 511 characters) and replace with the EXACT new substring. No other sentences, paragraphs, or fields are changed.

Anchor context on both sides is preserved verbatim: the preceding sentence "The r=0.92 is real, but it indicates shared causation, not independent prediction." remains unchanged; the following sentence "The dome’s contribution is to relabel the shared cause “Sub-terrestrial structure”" remains unchanged. The new bridge text reconciles "shared causation" (prior sentence — conceded for the sake of argument) with "two distinct proximate mechanisms" (our push-back) by introducing "shared regional context" at the regional/tectonic level while separating the proximate causes.

Follow-on grammar check: the unchanged next sentence still reads naturally because the new text closes with "shared regional geology", which the dome-label critique can still attack as the dome's relabeling of "shared cause" into "Sub-terrestrial structure".
patch_a_old_substring: Standard geophysics supplies the shared cause: East Antarctic crustal roots plus the uncompensated sub-lithospheric mantle plume (Pappa et al., 2019, JGR) produce both the crustal gravity low via isostatic imbalance and, through the associated lithospheric/mantle structure, contribute to the geometry of the SAA field minimum. The co-location of the two anomaly fields is a downstream consequence of common-cause geology, not a novel forecast.
patch_a_new_substring: Standard geophysics supplies the shared regional context but resolves it into two distinct proximate mechanisms, not a single shared cause. The Antarctic gravity low is explained by East Antarctic crustal roots plus the uncompensated sub-lithospheric mantle plume acting on crustal density structure via isostatic imbalance (Pappa et al., 2019, JGR). The South Atlantic Anomaly is independently explained as a core-dynamo phenomenon — reverse-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary documented in the CHAOS-7 geomagnetic field model (Finlay et al., 2020), and attributed to long-lived heterogeneity at the base of the mantle (the African Large Low Shear Velocity Province) by Tarduno et al. (2015). The two anomalies share a region of deep geological complexity — the African LLSVP footprint influences both overlying lithospheric structure and core-surface flow — but have distinct physical causes, a lithospheric/mantle density feature on one side and a core-surface flux feature on the other, and the dome supplies an equation for neither. The co-location of the two fields is a downstream consequence of shared regional geology, not a novel forecast.
holes_addressed: WIN-067.c10 H1 (MODERATE / advocate-survives-4): Pappa et al., 2019 was cited as the shared-cause explanation for BOTH the Antarctic gravity anomaly AND the SAA field minimum. Pappa addresses Antarctic crustal/mantle gravity structure; it does not address the core-dynamo phenomenon that produces the SAA. The patch splits the citation: Pappa 2019 stays scoped to the gravity anomaly, and two mainstream SAA-specific references are added — CHAOS-7 geomagnetic field model (Finlay et al., 2020; DOI 10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9, already canonical elsewhere in this file) for the current-era reverse-flux-patch description, and Tarduno et al., 2015 Nature Communications (DOI 10.1038/ncomms8865, already canonical elsewhere in this file) for the deep-mantle (African LLSVP) control on the anomaly. The reworded linking phrase explicitly states the two anomalies share a region of deep geological complexity (the LLSVP footprint influencing both overlying lithospheric structure and core-surface flow) but have distinct physical causes, and reiterates that the dome supplies an equation for neither — converting the defender's strongest counter into a reinforcing point (mainstream geophysics has TWO mechanisms where the dome has zero equations).
new_evidence_added: Finlay et al., 2020 — CHAOS-7 geomagnetic field model, Earth Planets Space 72:156, DOI 10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9. Canonical mainstream reference for current-era SAA evolution via reverse-flux patches at the CMB. Already cited elsewhere in wins.json with identical link text and DOI (WIN-032 context), so no new citation style or unverified DOI introduced.

Tarduno et al., 2015 — Nature Communications, "Antiquity of the South Atlantic Anomaly and evidence for top-down control on the geodynamo", DOI 10.1038/ncomms8865. Canonical reference for African LLSVP base-of-mantle heterogeneity as the deep driver of SAA recurrence. Already cited elsewhere in wins.json with identical link text and DOI (WIN-032 context).

Explicit tectonic-level framing: "shared regional context" (LLSVP footprint) resolved into two distinct proximate mechanisms (lithospheric/mantle density for gravity; core-surface flux for SAA). Replaces the previous "through the associated lithospheric/mantle structure" hand-wave that the c10 advocate correctly identified as overreach of Pappa's scope into the magnetic-field domain.

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender: "You have added two citations (Finlay, Tarduno) for the SAA but still have not supplied a dome-geometry equation — you are just citing mainstream science harder. The dome's r=0.92 remains an empirical observation that mainstream science did not publish as a specific prediction. Give him credit for identifying it."
anticipated_objections.response: Acknowledged — the kernel-of-truth concession on r=0.92 is preserved verbatim (the paragraph still opens with "The r=0.92 is real" and calls it a "genuinely high empirical regularity"). The patch does not contest the correlation itself; it contests the dome's ownership of the mechanism. The revised text shows that mainstream science has quantitative models of BOTH fields independently — Pappa for gravity, Finlay 2020 + Tarduno 2015 for the SAA — collectively predicting regional co-location that has been measured. The dome supplies zero equations mapping disc geometry to either field, so the observation is downstream common-cause geography, not a dome-specific forecast. Credit for noticing the regularity is appropriate; credit for predicting it is not earned.

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender: "You say 'shared causation' in one sentence and 'not a single shared cause' in the next. Which is it?"
anticipated_objections.response: Standard geophysics resolves the ambiguity by distinguishing scales. At the regional/tectonic scale the two anomalies share a common context: both sit above the African LLSVP, a mantle-scale heterogeneity that influences overlying lithospheric structure (driving the gravity anomaly) and core-surface flow (driving the SAA). At the proximate-mechanism scale these are two distinct physical systems — crustal isostasy vs core-dynamo reverse-flux patches — with separate equations and independent observational constraints. "Shared causation" (prior sentence, conceded for argument) refers to the regional-context layer; "two distinct proximate mechanisms" (new text) refers to the mechanism layer. The dome flattens both into a single undifferentiated label; mainstream geophysics resolves them.

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender: "If both anomalies are caused by the LLSVP footprint, then Tarduno 2015 IS a shared-cause citation — you have just replaced one shared cause (Pappa) with another (LLSVP)."
anticipated_objections.response: The LLSVP is a regional context — a deep-mantle heterogeneity that spatially co-locates the two fields — not a shared mechanism. Tarduno 2015 invokes the LLSVP as the top-down control on the geodynamo (producing reverse-flux patches at the CMB, which give rise to the SAA); Pappa 2019 invokes East Antarctic crustal roots plus a sub-lithospheric mantle plume (producing the gravity anomaly via isostatic imbalance). These are two different physical processes operating in two different Earth layers; the LLSVP is a regional influence on both but does not itself supply the gravity or magnetic equation. By contrast the dome's "Sub-terrestrial structure" label supplies no mechanism at any scale and no equation at any layer — the asymmetry is that mainstream geophysics has distinguishable equations while the dome has zero.

kernel_of_truth: The Mascon ↔ SAA r=0.92 correlation is a genuinely high empirical regularity between two independently measured global anomaly fields — mainstream science did not publish this specific correlation coefficient as a prediction, so the dome author's identification of the relationship is worth acknowledging (the kernel is preserved: the paragraph still opens with "The r=0.92 is real"). Additionally, the dome is correct that the two fields ARE physically related through sub-terrestrial structure — mainstream geophysics says the same thing, but resolves it into layered mechanisms (lithosphere for gravity, core-mantle boundary for SAA, African LLSVP as the shared deep-mantle context) rather than an undifferentiated label.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_claim: A high correlation between two independently measured anomaly fields indicates shared context, not shared mechanism, and certainly not a dome-specific forecast. Mainstream geophysics supplies TWO independent proximate mechanisms — Pappa 2019 for the crustal/mantle gravity anomaly and Finlay 2020 + Tarduno 2015 for the core-dynamo reverse-flux-patch SAA — linked by a common regional context (the African LLSVP). The dome supplies an equation for neither mechanism. "Sub-terrestrial structure" is a single-word placeholder that flattens a multi-layer geophysical story into a relabel. The r=0.92 was measured, not predicted.

testing.prior_sentence_preserved: The r=0.92 is real, but it indicates shared causation, not independent prediction.
testing.following_sentence_preserved: The dome’s contribution is to relabel the shared cause “Sub-terrestrial structure” without supplying any equation mapping dome geometry (disc radius, H(r), aetheric parameters) to either field — so r=0.92 is empirical regularity, not forecast.
